title: Learning to Automate: How Multi-Site Firms Distribute Exploration and Exploitation Across Facilities
data_source: Company X (Pitney Bowes): 8,698 daily PPH observations, 20 facilities, 21 automation projects, 69 interviews

categories.framing: Purely descriptive: seasonal staffing and facility productivity patterns

events.detail: Early exploration of PPH data revealed heterogeneous patterns across facilities. No theoretical framing yet—just documenting what the data show.

categories.mechanism: Turnover spreads across facilities through network effects

events.detail: Attempted to frame around turnover spreading across facilities. Abandoned: this was one finding, not the full story of what's happening in the data.

categories.framing: Seasonal paradox: productivity patterns don't match staffing patterns

events.detail: Attempted to frame around seasonal staffing patterns. Abandoned: staffing isn't the main story—automation is.
events.trigger: Realized seasonal effects were secondary to automation effects

categories.framing: Automation architectures: documenting heterogeneity in automation outcomes
categories.evidence: Identified systematic patterns: same tech → different outcomes across facilities
categories.claims: Automation outcomes are heterogeneous, not uniform

events.detail: Documented that identical technologies produce systematically different outcomes across facilities. Still descriptive—no theoretical mechanism explaining why.
events.trigger: Automation project data integrated with PPH data

categories.mechanism: Tested Thompson (1967) buffering: do facilities buffer against environmental volatility?
categories.claims: REJECTED BY DATA: Volume variance test showed expansion and efficiency nodes have identical CV (~0.59)

events.detail: Tested whether facilities differentiate by buffering against volatility (Thompson 1967). Data rejected this: volume variance is identical across facility types (CV ~0.59). Needed different theoretical frame.

categories.mechanism: Evidence found in qual data: 'Gallatin develops, Fresno perfects'—intentional role differentiation
categories.evidence: Qualitative evidence of deliberate role assignment across facilities

events.summary: Qual data reveals intentional role differentiation
events.detail: Searched qualitative data (69 interviews) for evidence of intentional facility differentiation. Found explicit language: 'Gallatin develops, Fresno perfects.' Managers deliberately assign learning roles to different facilities.

categories.evidence: Facility size × automation interaction: statistically significant (p=0.001)
categories.claims: Larger facilities show worse automation outcomes (coordination costs)
categories.boundaries: Size as moderator: smaller facilities adapt better

events.detail: Tested facility size as moderator of automation effects. Significant interaction (p=0.001): larger facilities show less favorable productivity responses. Adds 'coordination costs of scale' as boundary condition.

categories.framing: Spatial ambidexterity: firms solve exploration-exploitation dilemma across geography
categories.mechanism: Multi-site firms deliberately assign learning roles (pilot/optimization/scale/shrink) to different facilities. Pooling heterogeneous roles produces null aggregate effects.
categories.evidence: Three-role pattern: optimization (+50% PPH), scale (volume growth), shrink (decline). Only 1 optimization facility, 2 scale, 4 shrink.
categories.claims: NEW: Heterogeneous effects by role explain null pooled effects. NEW: Spatial separation as fourth mode of ambidexterity (alongside structural, temporal, contextual).
categories.boundaries: N=1 optimization facility. Size moderation. Ex-post role classification.
categories.robustness: DiD with facility and month FE. Pre-trend tests. Threshold robustness (9 combinations).

events.detail: Integrated qualitative evidence of intentional role assignment with quantitative pattern of heterogeneous effects. Core insight: firms solve exploration-exploitation dilemma spatially—pilot nodes explore, optimization nodes exploit. Pooling heterogeneous roles produces the misleading 'null effect of automation' that prior research finds.
events.trigger: Qualitative evidence of 'Gallatin develops, Fresno perfects' provided mechanism

categories.validation: ZIP CRITIQUE: Packaged learning_to_automate_FULL_PACKAGE_20251126.zip for external model review
categories.workflow_refinement: Zip critique process: package manuscript + data + code, send to external AI for rigorous adversarial review

events.summary: Zip critique: sent to external model
events.detail: Packaged full paper + data + code into learning_to_automate_FULL_PACKAGE_20251126.zip and sent to powerful external AI model for rigorous adversarial review. Package included: main.tex, PPH data, run_all_robustness_checks.py, Project Listing.xlsx, DATA_DESCRIPTOR.md, REVIEW_PROMPT.md.

categories.claims: Consolidated propositions: 5 → 3. Built up null hypothesis as sensible.
categories.validation: Friendly review + Zuckerman evaluation completed. Score: 9/10 criteria met. Central vulnerability: ex-post role classification.

events.detail: Ran Zuckerman criteria evaluation (9/10 after revisions). Key changes: (1) Consolidated 5 propositions to 3 for memorability; (2) Built up null hypothesis ('uniform effects are not naive—they reflect sensible reasoning'); (3) Made 'spatial ambidexterity' the memorable term. Friendly review identified central vulnerability: ex-post role classification.
events.trigger: Sent to external AI reviewer; ran /eval-zuckerman

events.version_name: QUANTITATIVE HALLUCINATION: Placebo Test Bug Discovered

categories.evidence: HALLUCINATION: Placebo test was producing meaningless results—all 1000 iterations identical
categories.robustness: BROKEN: Placebo test variance = 0. Bug: mapping = dict(zip(dates.index, shuffled)) returned same dates every iteration
categories.validation: External model review ran actual code and detected bug by examining output variance—internal review had accepted reported p-value without verification

events.summary: HALLUCINATION: Placebo test code was broken
events.detail: External zip critique revealed critical code bug: the placebo test appeared to run 1,000 permutation iterations but produced identical effects every time (variance = 0). Bug in line: 'mapping = dict(zip(dates.index, shuffled))' — merge operation returned same dates. The reported 'p-value' was meaningless. This would have been published without external code verification.
events.trigger: External model ran actual code and checked output variance

categories.boundaries: N=1 optimization facility explicitly acknowledged. 'Shrink' nodes connected back to 'pilot' role.
categories.robustness: 5 issues identified: broken placebo, n=1 optimization, low-power pre-trends, undocumented size moderation, outcome-based role classification
categories.validation: Major review identified 5 vulnerabilities. Verdict: publishable for OrgSci/ASQ; pessimistic for ManSci

events.detail: Comprehensive external review (Dec 9) identified 5 critical issues: (1) placebo test broken (see p2-011); (2) only 1 optimization facility; (3) pre-trends tests low-power; (4) size moderation undocumented; (5) role classification outcome-based. Verdict: publishable for OrgSci/ASQ with tightening; pessimistic for ManSci.

categories.validation: Critique verification completed: confirmed placebo bug is real, n=1 optimization is correct, pre-trends insufficient

events.detail: Independent verification of reviewer claims against actual code/data. Confirmed: (1) placebo test bug is real; (2) only 1 optimization facility is correct; (3) pre-trends are insufficient for optimization. Partially confirmed: size interaction code exists separately but not in main robustness script.

categories.validation: Comprehensive audit completed: 3,949 evidence items linked to 9 claims across 69 interviews
categories.replication: Living Paper infrastructure: claims.jsonl, evidence.jsonl, links.csv created

events.summary: Claims audit and Living Paper integration
events.detail: Ran comprehensive audit linking all claims to qualitative evidence. Created Living Paper artifacts: 9 claims, 3,949 evidence items, verification checklist. Audit found core narrative well-supported; 3 claims need revision (CLM-006, CLM-007, CLM-009 have weak/challenging evidence).

categories.evidence: Key finding from audit: Site selection driven by proximity to vendor more than strategic role assignment

events.summary: Site selection revision based on audit
events.detail: Audit revealed: 'Normally they do pick a site because of the proximity of location to the vendor.' This challenges claim of purely strategic role assignment. Revised CLM-006 to acknowledge pragmatic location factors alongside deliberate selection.
Created validation package for external sharing. Includes robustness scripts, threshold analysis, pre-trend tests. Overleaf export also created.
Commit 164da3e: 'Initial commit: warehouse automation papers (manuscripts only)'
Commit f8e0839: 'Fix sentence-start capitalization in Learning to Automate paper'

categories.claims: Softened: 'validated this framework by testing' → 'checked whether...aligned'; 'validate that the empirical clusters' → 'check whether...clusters'
categories.register: Systematic removal of hypothesis-testing language; aligned with inductive genre

events.detail: Commit 78cb070: Systematic replacement of hypothesis-testing language with theory-building alternatives. 'validate' → 'check whether'; 'test' → 'examine'. Rationale: theory-building papers discover patterns; they don't test hypotheses.

categories.boundaries: N=1 optimization facility acknowledged in text. Role terminology footnote added. Outlier sign flip made explicit. Winsorizing added to event study.
categories.robustness: Pre-trend narrative nuanced (low-power acknowledged). Seasonality controls added to analysis.

events.detail: Addressed reviewer feedback: (1) n=1 optimization facility now explicit; (2) role terminology footnote distinguishes 'pilot' from 'optimization'; (3) pre-trend narrative acknowledges low power; (4) seasonality controls added; (5) outlier sign flip explained; (6) winsorizing added to event study.
Final main.tex prepared (80 KB, Jan 13). Ready for submission to Organization Science.

events.version_name: Theory-Forge Quality Gate F: Conditional Pass

categories.register: Genre evaluation: PASS. Discovery framing appropriate for OrgSci. Fixed 'derive observable implications' → 'develop the theoretical framework'.
categories.validation: Gates A-D PASSED. Gate F CONDITIONAL: Becker PASS, Genre PASS, Limitations REVISE (560 words, 6 enumerated items → need 2 paragraphs), Citations CONDITIONAL (29 vs 50 minimum for OrgSci).

events.summary: Theory-forge Gate F: conditional pass, needs citation/limitation fixes
events.detail: Feb 4, 2026: Ran full theory-forge evaluation pipeline including Becker, genre, limitations, and citations checks. Gate D PASSED (Zuckerman 9/10, Becker PASS, Genre PASS). Gate F CONDITIONAL: limitations section needs consolidation (6 enumerated items → 2 paragraphs), citation count below OrgSci minimum (29 vs 50). Both fixable pre-submission.
events.trigger: Theory-forge quality gates applied to mature manuscript
